title: Finding Things
scenario: A legacy configuration file, an orphaned log file larger than 50KB, and a custom shell helper script are scattered somewhere in the system. You need to find their locations and write their full absolute paths into four specific output files.
symptoms: Scattered config, log, and script files
Missing output path files in home directory
mission: Use 'find' and 'which' to locate the target files and save their absolute paths to ~/found_config.txt, ~/found_largelog.txt, ~/found_script.txt, and ~/bash_location.txt.

hints.title: Finding Config
hints.content: You can find custom config files using `find /etc -name '*custom_app*'`.

hints.title: Finding Large Logs
hints.content: Use size options with find, such as `find /var/log -size +50k` to locate the orphaned log file.

hints.title: Saving the paths
hints.content: Save paths by redirecting output: e.g. `find /etc -name 'custom_app.conf' > /home/intern/found_config.txt` and `which bash > /home/intern/bash_location.txt`.

hints.title: Bonus: locate command
hints.content: Bonus: Try `locate custom_app.conf` to find the same file using the locate index. Note: you may need to run `updatedb` first.
